client_context.facility_line: Hamburg Bakery & Rietberg Cold Cuts, Federal Republic of Germany
client_context.jurisdiction_label: EU & German Sustainability Frameworks for Food Manufacturers

executive_summary:
January 2026 opens with Gerold & Team's ESRS double materiality assessment formally underway, led by BDO Hamburg with internal sustainability coordinator Lea MÃžller managing data collection across both facilities. The preliminary DMA stakeholder engagement sessions conducted in January identified 47 internal and external stakeholders, with Hamburg Bakery's retail customers and Rietberg's agricultural cooperative suppliers as the two most engaged external stakeholder groups.

The DE-VerpackDG-2026 consultation period is progressing, with BVE circulating a draft industry response on 15 January 2026. Gerold's packaging team has reviewed the draft and confirmed two areas of concern specific to the company: (1) the PackML declaration deadline of 30 June 2026 requires Gerold to digitise its existing paper-based packaging material records, which will require a data entry project of approximately 40 staff-hours; and (2) the transit packaging EPR extension proposed in the amendment could capture Gerold's Hamburg wholesale distribution boxes if the final text adopts the broad definition proposed in Article 3(7a).

Freshfields' soy feed supply chain assessment for EUDR purposes is progressing. Initial responses from Westfleisch confirm that their contracted farms use approximately 68% Brazilian soy in feed formulations, sourced through three commodity traders. Westfleisch has indicated it will be able to provide plot-level geolocation data for the Brazilian soy farms by March 2026 through its participation in the Farm Sustainability Assessment (FSA) programme managed by the Sustainable Agriculture Initiative Platform (SAI Platform).

sections.heading: Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive â DMA Underway
paragraphs: DMA formally underway with BDO Hamburg. 47 stakeholders identified; retail customers and agricultural suppliers as primary external groups. E1 (bakery oven gas) and E3 (cold cuts water) are emerging as the highest-priority environmental topics; S2 (soy feed supply chain) may become material if EUDR supply chain mapping reveals systematic deforestation risks.
sections.callout: Complete stakeholder materiality survey and DMA first assessment by 31 March 2026.

sections.heading: VerpackungsdurchfÃžhrungsgesetz Amendment
paragraphs: BVE draft industry response circulated 15 January 2026. Gerold confirmed two specific concerns: (1) PackML declaration digitisation requires ~40 staff-hours by June 2026; (2) Article 3(7a) transit packaging EPR extension could capture Hamburg wholesale distribution boxes. BVE response deadline for member input: 5 February 2026.
sections.callout: Submit Gerold-specific comments to BVE by 5 February 2026; begin PackML packaging data digitisation project by 28 February 2026.
